ks1.objective: Roll your hoop and try to make it land around one of the scattered objects.
successCriteria: Hoop rolls in the right direction

lks2.objective: Develop hoop-rolling accuracy — control power and direction to land your hoop around scattered equipment.
successCriteria: Beats own count over the round
Stays patient and resets after misses

space.description: Throwing line at one end. Equipment scattered in front of the line.

steps: Split the class into teams of 2–3.
Spread equipment out randomly in front of the throwing line.
Children take turns to roll their hoop and try to land it around an object.
If successful, collect that object for your team.
Play until all equipment has been collected — most objects wins.
questions: What strategies can you come up with as a team?
How are you controlling the power and speed of your hoop roll to make it land accurately?
How do you stay patient and keep trying if your hoop doesn't land around the equipment?

step.space: Move the equipment closer or further from the throwing line
step.task: Children can throw their hoops instead of rolling
step.equipment: Use a ball to roll and hit the equipment instead
step.people: Have blockers who can move between the equipment
